free_text: Photoneutron cross sections for 64Zn and 68Zn in  the
The multichannel method of the reaction  yield
measurement  was  used  in  which the bremsstrahlung
end-point energy has been changed  automatically  in
each cycle of electron acceleration in betatron with
The metallic zinc (64Zn) and zinc oxide (68ZnO)  were
The  Penfold  - Leiss unfolding method was used
for obtaining cross-section data  from  experimental
The total photoneutron cross sections  for  64Zn  and
68Zn were  obtained.  The  measurements were carried
out from the threshold to 27 MeV in  50  keV  steps.
The cross  sections  show an obvious structure.  The
half width of the curves is  about  10  MeV. If  the
multiple production  is  not  considered,  then  the
integrated cross sections for 64Zn and 68Zn are  800
(80) and 1630 (160) MeV x mb.  The experimental data
were compared   with   predictions   of   collective
dynamical theory  and  with  the  concept of isospin
splitting of the giant dipole resonance.
Data presented  in Fig. 1 of article are photoneutron
(g,1n1p) in accordance with the reaction spelling in
text  (page 233, right column, line 8 from the top),
not total photoneutron cross sections  as written in
the line 6 and in the caption to Fig. 1.
Data were compiled  at  the  MSU   SINP
SD: Ref. on transl. was added.
